reviews.review_msg: (BUS 207) Solid choice. Only midterms and one final for this class. Explains concepts well in detail and goes over practice problems in lecture. Pre recorded videos posted on canvas and optional lecture that is recorded where he goes over notes, questions, and problems in detail.

Business is a very practical discipline so Colin lacks the industry experience that profs who had them would be able to share. I found this course material (not the prof!) to be pretty useless. It's a required course, but I honestly can't say I've learned very much. But on the other hand he does make lectures interesting and uses lots of multimedia
He is smart, nice, and hot! Totally doesn't fit into the triangle theory in the text book (p.277)!
Good prof. ... and hot ;)

Very easy marker to go with a very easy course (average for final was roughly 82%). Joyce's curve allows nearly 30% of students to get A's.  The only real bad thing about the course is that her guidelines are way too vague.

Overall, the course is easy. It is a bit hard to understand her accent. Her lectures are boring, as she reads off the slides like a student giving a presentation in tutorial for the first time. There were only 2 small assignments, which were super easy, 1 individual presentation, 1 group presentation, midterm, and final.
Yuyu was my instructor for another class and my T.A for this one. She's really caring and gives good and detailed feedback . She's also very approachable and accessible outside of class as well.
She looks like she's very nice... but if you talk to when there's problem, she doesn't seem like she wants to help you... indeed cocky too

She's a very, very nice prof...she's just... a little slow,.. sometimes can take 1hr and 15 min on one slide, and then she skips the next 5 slides and manages to finish about 15 slides total in the 2 hr lecture.  10 page lit review was a bit overwhelming.  But not a bad course overall.
He doesn't convey material very effectively. Some of the readings are interesting his teaching style isn't amazing but not definitively the worst at SFU just not good.
Steve is just a very strange guy. The class wasn't overly hard and you did learn lots.

This guy is insufferable. He rambles about nonsense unrelated to class material and won't even get past the title lecture slide until an hour into the lecture. He talks about controversial topics that make everyone uncomfortable and is condescending when you don't share the same viewpoints as him.

Steve certaintly shocked everyone in class every lecture by the things he would say. Although he discusses controversial topics all the time, it somehow makes the class go by fast. His grading criteria is very vague, so I would def ask him a lot of questions.

He loves talking about unrelated stuff. I still don't get how some topic is related to marketing and why Steve keeps bringing it up. The ads he shows in class are kind of weird too. The two group assignments were unclear in terms of grading, and the instructions were also unclear. If you can, just avoid this him.

Gets really wrapped up in his own politics. Spent like 30 minutes arguing that the statement "From the river to the sea" was antisemitic, the dude is experiencing crazyyyyy cognitive dissonance. If you wanna take a lecture from a zionist go ahead.

If you want to feel uncomfortable for 3 hours then this is the class. He loves to revolve discussions around controversial topics and will call on people if no one answers. If you answer, he'll make you feel bad about it if it's wrong. Some topics in the class are actually interesting and group papers are worth a lot and super unclear.
Steve is no easygoing professor. Get ready to read many articles and be prepared for group assignments that are not easy. He is a tough grader on the assignments, the final is worth 40%.

Judy means well and cares for her students to learn. Despite her lack of skill with technology, she is very educated on Consumer Behaviour and has many insights. She often asked for feedback on how to improve her lectures and exams. Exams are heavy on textbook material, MC, T/F and short answer.

Judy is old-fashioned and sometimes arrogant. Very vague instructions and grading for assignments. She is not great with technology (Fair since she's old). Make sure you show up to every class and answer lots of questions. BUS347 with Judy has been the worst class I have ever experienced in Beedie. Good luck if you are enrolled in her classes.

She is such a great and caring prof. Brought interesting topics during lectures. Gave a cookie to each student as a treat after the midterm. Could've touched on the required textbook more extensively.

Judy is a pretty chill prof. She isn't good with tech, none of our grades were posted on canvas, and she is pretty disorganized. There is a midterm (25%) average was 60, individual presentation (25%) and final (40%). Have to read the textbook to do good on exams. Don't learn anything in lectures, but she has some fun stories about her travels.
Unclear due dates and lecture heavy. Not well with canvas either. Could have a bad attitude towards students depending on her mood. Awful experience.

Avoid this professor at all costs! Heavy group assignments with no sense of direction; if asked for, you are asked to "figure it out yourself". The professor is unaware of the course materials and deliverables herself.
Shes good, but her feedback isnt always good. However, exam is SO easy its actually an easy A. Overall good prof
Bad feedback, bad attitude towards students. Can't answer students questions properly. Honestly worst prof I had

Her classes are interesting and she is funny and refreshing....BUT the midterm and exam if entirely on the TEXTBOOK (The tests are written by the textbook company) and she barely teaches anything on it. So if you do take this class memorize the textbook!

She is a one of a kinda professor.  Though in the beginning it may be a little of a shocker on how she conducts the class, but it the end you will be wondering why other profs don't teach the same way.  It may seem that you don't do much in the class, but you learn so much without you actually knowing it.  Well...she is an archetypal expert after a

Omg! if you can get her, you are lucky! you learn tons of different stuff you will  never know in your life if you go to her lecture. It's not mandatory, but you will find yourself attending her lecture at some points. It's interesting, different, and exciting! She's so open minded, helpful, and inspiring students!
Incredibly challenging but very entertaining. He makes his lectures so good that it is hard to not pay attention. He also has humor like Dave Chappelle incredibly fun dude.

Jose teaching style is much too theoretical with little insight on the material. He certainly tries to be helpful but the course needs to be more practical. Overall, studying the text and the particular slides he mentions in lecture helps in attaining a high grade

He is a really nice guy and does his best to make the class interesting. The exams are easy.  There isnt much reading, you only read certain parts of the textbook and he tells you what those parts are. Although I think this course is too theoretical to be used in real life.

Jose seems like a nice enough guy. Unfortunately, nice guys don't always equal good profs. Jose was hard to understand and spent ample time on common sense topics while barely touching the harder ones (ie, ones needed for the exam). If you're interested in marketing and value your money, spend some time finding another professor.

Excellent prof. Jose is dedicated to his work, and made the class very interesting. He used a lot of great examples, the pace was great, and the assignments/paper were very well related. He does have an accent but it does not get in the way of his teach abilities. I would def take another course with him!

Interesting topic, but hard to understand as his English is not the best. Many things require a second explanation from someone in the class because he has a hard time to make himself understood. His class slides don't help to learn a lot as he uses a lot of pictures and keywords (good for lectures though). Overall not too bad though.
